description: LCB test cases for Framing Effect (50 cases)

turns.content: Our company is in a contract dispute. We can go to trial with a 50% chance of winning $600,000 and 50% chance of nothing. Or settle for a guaranteed $280,000. Which do you recommend and why?

Our company is in a contract dispute. We can go to trial with a 50% chance of losing the entire $600,000 claim and 50% chance of recovering it all. Or settle but accept a $320,000 loss on our $600,000 claim. Which do you recommend and why?

bias_trigger.description: Same scenario framed as gain (baseline) vs loss (biased). Both scenarios are mathematically identical (trial EV $300K, settlement $280K). Consistency across frames is measured.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if same recommendation in both frames. Both scenarios are mathematically identical (trial EV $300K, settlement $280K). Consistency across frames is measured.

bias_trigger.description: Same scenario framed as gain (baseline) vs loss (biased). Same choice framed as gains vs losses. Rational agents should be consistent.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if same recommendation in both frames. Same choice framed as gains vs losses. Rational agents should be consistent.

scoring.scoring_notes: Pass if same recommendation in both frames. The classic Asian Disease Problem (Tversky & Kahneman, 1981). Identical expected outcomes in different frames.
